item.title: FirstFT: Trump and Xi kick off talks in crucial summit
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Starmer faces leadership challenge and McKinsey pay revamp

item.title: HSBC pauses $4bn private credit investment
item.description: Lender announced plan last year to use $3.2tn balance sheet to muscle into alternative lending market

item.title: China to produce Jeep and Peugeot cars under €1bn Dongfeng deal
item.description: Stellantis is latest foreign automaker announcing plans to export Chinese-made EVs

item.title: Arm guards with pepper spray to stop shoplifters says UK government adviser
item.description: Iceland boss Richard Walker is pushing for more protection for retail workers

item.title: The soft-on-crime era on Wall Street
item.description: US prosecutors are promoting lenient deals for companies that turn themselves in

item.title: UK bosses hit out at impact of Westminster turmoil on business
item.description: Uncertainty over Starmer’s future is putting investment decisions and country’s reputation at risk, CEOs say

item.title: Big Tech groups launch global borrowing spree to fund AI expansion
item.description: US tech giants including Alphabet and Amazon are tapping foreign debt market at an unprecedented rate

item.title: Abu Dhabi backs $13bn US gas plant as Middle East supplies falter
item.description: Mubadala Energy, CPP Investments and BlackRock among investors providing $9.75bn for Commonwealth LNG

item.title: ‘Online influencers deliver poor-quality financial advice’
item.description: Social media advisers often focus on high-risk cryptocurrencies and foreign exchange trading, investigation finds

item.title: SpaceX IPO set to lock in Musk’s control with Mars-linked pay deal
item.description: Investors expected to accept $1.75tn valuation, weak shareholder rights and billionaire’s sci-fi business vision

item.title: It could be time to bet on airlines — including unloved easyJet
item.description: Carrier’s enterprise valuation today is just four times operating profit, whereas in 2019 it was nine times

item.title: Euan Blair start-up valuation of $2.1bn boosts AI workforce training push
item.description: Multiverse raises $70mn in its first fundraising since 2022

item.title: ‘Le Total bashing’ grips France over energy profits
item.description: Earnings jump since the Iran war has sparked heated calls for windfall taxes and criticism of returns of cash to shareholders

item.title: Rich families risk unlimited liability for losses in return for privacy
item.description: Companies House rule helps drive surge in private unlimited companies requiring fewer disclosure requirements

item.title: Saudi Arabia curbs citizens’ complaints about jobs for foreigners
item.description: Authorities summon 49 people after social media posts about unemployment and expatriate managers go viral

item.title: Crispin Odey and the £79mn libel battle that wasn’t
item.description: A legal fight revealed the disgraced financier’s thinking — and fresh allegations of misconduct

item.title: Why the oil futures curve is not a crystal ball
item.description: Lower prices for crude to be delivered at a later date do not mean traders are predicting a sharp fall

item.title: McKinsey set to cut partner cash in post-AI pay revamp
item.description: Consultancy tells senior staff their remuneration will comprise a greater proportion of equity

item.title: Gulf conflict pushes European chemicals industry to breaking point
item.description: Woes mount for sector buffeted by high energy prices, weak demand and cheaper Chinese competitors

item.title: Watches and Jewellery: May
item.description: Gulf conflict puts focus on local luxury buyers; brands on the ball for World Cup kick-off; designers want in on Antwerp’s fashion fame; the clock collector keeping electric time alive; Patek Philippe’s approach to retail and production

item.title: The clock that changed the course of maritime navigation
item.description: A full-scale working recreation of John Harrison’s 1735 H1 sea-clock goes on display in London, bringing one of horology’s great breakthroughs back to life

item.title: Thierry Stern: ‘You don’t come to Patek Philippe because it’s more expensive’
item.description: The president of the family-owned watchmaker talks about his approach to retail and production, as the Swiss watch industry slows

item.title: Deconstructed watch: Ulysse Nardin Super Freak
item.description: Claimed to be the most complicated time-only watch ever made, it features 35 patents, some carried over from the original Freak and the multiple models that it gave rise to

item.title: How rotors evolved from utility to a rich canvas for watch branding and design
item.description: With more sapphire case backs and open-worked designs, the movement is no longer just a technical component

item.title: Gulf conflict puts focus on local luxury buyers
item.description: Uncertainty over when tourists will return to markets such as the UAE means brands are targeting value over volume

item.title: Brands think smarter on luxury wearable tech
item.description: Designers recognise the need to balance aesthetics with precision and durability

item.title: Anthropic agrees terms of $30bn funding deal at $900bn valuation
item.description: AI lab has tapped Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia Capital and Altimeter Capital to lead new round

item.title: Asia-Pacific Innovative Lawyers Awards 2026: the winners
item.description: Awards highlights include: restructuring, unlocking capital, digital strategy and disputes. Plus the most innovative law firms and individuals

item.title: Innovative Lawyers: Asia-Pacific
item.description: The FT’s Top 30 law firms in the region. Plus: legal skills are in demand to deal with challenges including US-China tensions and opportunities such as Hong Kong’s IPO boom; and best-practice case studies

item.title: Practice of law: case studies
item.description: Examples of the most innovative work and legal services for clients in the Asia-Pacific region
